content.text: I found the likely RESETSTAT command implementation in `src/config.c`, reset helper comments in `src/server.c`/`src/cluster.c`, and existing cluster slot-stat tests in `tests/unit/cluster/slot-stats.tcl`. Next I’ll inspect those areas to identify where cluster bus counters should be reset and how to add a temporary regression test.

content.content:
130	
131	proc assert_slot_stats_monotonic_ascent {slot_stats orderby} {
132	    assert_slot_stats_monotonic_order $slot_stats $orderby 0
133	}
134	
135	proc wait_for_replica_key_exists {key key_count} {
136	    wait_for_condition 1000 50 {
137	        [R 1 exists $key] eq "$key_count"
138	    } else {
139	        fail "Test key was not replicated"
140	    }
141	}
142	
143	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
144	# Test cases for CLUSTER SLOT-STATS cpu-usec metric correctness.
145	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
146	
147	start_cluster 1 0 {tags {external:skip cluster} overrides {cluster-slot-stats-enabled yes}} {
148	
149	    # Define shared variables.
150	    set key "FOO"
151	    set key_slot [R 0 cluster keyslot $key]
152	    set key_secondary "FOO2"
153	    set key_secondary_slot [R 0 cluster keyslot $key_secondary]
154	    set metrics_to_assert [list cpu-usec]
155	
156	    test "CLUSTER SLOT-STATS cpu-usec reset upon CONFIG RESETSTAT." {
157	        R 0 SET $key VALUE
158	        R 0 DEL $key
159	        R 0 CONFIG RESETSTAT
160	        set slot_stats [R 0 CLUSTER SLOT-STATS SLOTSRANGE 0 16383]
161	        assert_empty_slot_stats $slot_stats $metrics_to_assert
162	    }
163	    R 0 CONFIG RESETSTAT
164	    R 0 FLUSHALL
165	
166	    test "CLUSTER SLOT-STATS cpu-usec reset upon slot migration." {
167	        R 0 SET $key VALUE
168	
169	        R 0 CLUSTER DELSLOTS $key_slot
170	        set slot_stats [R 0 CLUSTER SLOT-STATS SLOTSRANGE 0 16383]
171	        assert_empty_slot_stats $slot_stats $metrics_to_assert
172	
173	        R 0 CLUSTER ADDSLOTS $key_slot
174	        set slot_stats [R 0 CLUSTER SLOT-STATS SLOTSRANGE 0 16383]
175	        assert_empty_slot_stats $slot_stats $metrics_to_assert
176	    }
177	    R 0 CONFIG RESETSTAT
178	    R 0 FLUSHALL
179	
180	    test "CLUSTER SLOT-STATS cpu-usec for non-slot specific commands." {
181	        R 0 INFO
182	        set slot_stats [R 0 CLUSTER SLOT-STATS SLOTSRANGE 0 16383]
183	        assert_empty_slot_stats $slot_stats $metrics_to_assert
184	    }
185	    R 0 CONFIG RESETSTAT
186	    R 0 FLUSHALL
187	
188	    test "CLUSTER SLOT-STATS cpu-usec for slot specific commands." {
189	        R 0 SET $key VALUE

content.text: I found `CONFIG RESETSTAT` calls `resetClusterStats()`, but that function currently only resets slot stats; the bus message counters are initialized in `clusterInit()` and reported from cluster stats, so I’m going to locate the test helpers and INFO/CLUSTER output parsing next.

1	# Check cluster info stats
2	
3	start_cluster 2 0 {tags {external:skip cluster}} {
4	
5	test "Cluster should start ok" {
6	    wait_for_cluster_state ok
7	}
8	
9	set primary1 [srv 0 "client"]
10	set primary2 [srv -1 "client"]
11	
12	proc cmdstat {instance cmd} {
13	    return [cmdrstat $cmd $instance]
14	}
15	
16	proc errorstat {instance cmd} {
17	    return [errorrstat $cmd $instance]
18	}
19	
20	test "errorstats: rejected call due to MOVED Redirection" {
21	    $primary1 config resetstat
22	    $primary2 config resetstat
23	    assert_match {} [errorstat $primary1 MOVED]
24	    assert_match {} [errorstat $primary2 MOVED]
25	    # we know that one will have a MOVED reply and one will succeed
26	    catch {$primary1 set key b} replyP1
27	    catch {$primary2 set key b} replyP2
28	    # sort servers so we know which one failed
29	    if {$replyP1 eq {OK}} {
30	        assert_match {MOVED*} $replyP2
31	        set pok $primary1
32	        set perr $primary2
33	    } else {
34	        assert_match {MOVED*} $replyP1
35	        set pok $primary2
36	        set perr $primary1
37	    }
38	    assert_match {} [errorstat $pok MOVED]
39	    assert_match {*count=1*} [errorstat $perr MOVED]
40	    assert_match {*calls=0,*,rejected_calls=1,failed_calls=0} [cmdstat $perr set]
41	}
42	
43	} ;# start_cluster
44	
45	start_cluster 3 0 {tags {external:skip cluster} overrides {cluster-node-timeout 1000}} {
46	    test "fail reason changed" {
47	        # Kill one primary, so the cluster fail with not-full-coverage.
48	        pause_process [srv 0 pid]
49	        wait_for_condition 1000 50 {
50	            [CI 1 cluster_state] eq {fail} &&
51	            [CI 2 cluster_state] eq {fail}
52	        } else {
53	            fail "Cluster doesn't fail"
54	        }
55	        verify_log_message -1 "*At least one hash slot is not served by any available node*" 0
56	        verify_log_message -2 "*At least one hash slot is not served by any available node*" 0
57	
58	        # Kill one more primary, so the cluster fail with minority-partition.
59	        pause_process [srv -1 pid]
60	        wait_for_log_messages -2 {"*minority partition*"} 0 1000 50
61	
62	        resume_process [srv 0 pid]
63	        resume_process [srv -1 pid]
64	        wait_for_cluster_state ok
65	    }
66	}
67	
68	start_cluster 3 0 {tags {external:skip cluster} overrides {cluster-node-timeout 1000}} {
69	    # Kill two primaries to observe partial failure on the remaining one.
70	    pause_process [srv 0 pid]
71	    pause_process [srv -1 pid]
72	
73	    test "count - node partial failure" {
74	        wait_for_condition 500 10 {
75	            [CI 2 cluster_nodes_pfail] eq 2 &&
76	            [CI 2 cluster_nodes_fail] eq 0 &&
77	            [CI 2 cluster_voting_nodes_pfail] eq 2 &&
78	            [CI 2 cluster_voting_nodes_fail] eq 0
79	        } else {
80	            puts [R 2 CLUSTER INFO]
81	            fail "Node 0/1 never timed out"
82	        }
83	    }
84	
85	    # Enable one more primary to reach quorum about node 0 failure
86	    resume_process [srv -1 pid]
87	
88	    test "count - node complete failure" {
89	        # After reaching quorum about failure,
90	        # node 0 should be marked as FAIL across all nodes in the cluster
91	        wait_for_condition 100 100 {
92	            [CI 1 cluster_nodes_fail] eq 1 &&
93	            [CI 2 cluster_nodes_fail] eq 1 &&
94	            [CI 1 cluster_nodes_pfail] eq 0 &&
95	            [CI 2 cluster_nodes_pfail] eq 0 &&
96	            [CI 1 cluster_voting_nodes_fail] eq 1 &&
97	            [CI 2 cluster_voting_nodes_fail] eq 1
98	
99	        } else {
100	            fail "Node 0 never completely failed"
101	        }
102	    }
103	}
104

1	# Tcl cluster client as a wrapper of redis.rb.
2	# Copyright (c) 2014 Redis Ltd.
3	# Released under the BSD license like Redis itself
4	#
5	# Example usage:
6	#
7	# set c [valkey_cluster {127.0.0.1:6379 127.0.0.1:6380}]
8	# $c set foo
9	# $c get foo
10	# $c close
11	
12	package require Tcl 8.5
13	package provide valkey_cluster 0.1
14	
15	namespace eval valkey_cluster {}
16	set ::valkey_cluster::internal_id 0
17	set ::valkey_cluster::id 0
18	array set ::valkey_cluster::startup_nodes {}
19	array set ::valkey_cluster::nodes {}
20	array set ::valkey_cluster::slots {}
21	array set ::valkey_cluster::tls {}
22	
23	# List of "plain" commands, which are commands where the sole key is always
24	# the first argument.
25	set ::valkey_cluster::plain_commands {
26	    get set setnx setex psetex append strlen exists setbit getbit
27	    setrange getrange substr incr decr rpush lpush rpushx lpushx
28	    linsert rpop lpop brpop llen lindex lset lrange ltrim lrem
29	    sadd srem sismember smismember scard spop srandmember smembers sscan zadd
30	    zincrby zrem zremrangebyscore zremrangebyrank zremrangebylex zrange
31	    zrangebyscore zrevrangebyscore zrangebylex zrevrangebylex zcount
32	    zlexcount zrevrange zcard zscore zmscore zrank zrevrank zscan hset hsetnx
33	    hget hmset hmget hincrby hincrbyfloat hdel hlen hkeys hvals
34	    hgetall hexists hscan incrby decrby incrbyfloat getset move
35	    expire expireat pexpire pexpireat type ttl pttl persist restore
36	    dump bitcount bitpos pfadd pfcount cluster ssubscribe spublish
37	    sunsubscribe
38	}
39	
40	# Create a cluster client. The nodes are given as a list of host:port. The TLS
41	# parameter (1 or 0) is optional and defaults to the global $::tls.
42	proc valkey_cluster {nodes {tls -1}} {
43	    set id [incr ::valkey_cluster::id]
44	    set ::valkey_cluster::startup_nodes($id) $nodes
45	    set ::valkey_cluster::nodes($id) {}
46	    set ::valkey_cluster::slots($id) {}
47	    set ::valkey_cluster::tls($id) [expr $tls == -1 ? $::tls : $tls]
48	    set handle [interp alias {} ::valkey_cluster::instance$id {} ::valkey_cluster::__dispatch__ $id]
49	    $handle refresh_nodes_map
50	    return $handle
51	}
52	
53	# Totally reset the slots / nodes state for the client, calls
54	# CLUSTER NODES in the first startup node available, populates the
55	# list of nodes ::valkey_cluster::nodes($id) with an hash mapping node
56	# ip:port to a representation of the node (another hash), and finally
57	# maps ::valkey_cluster::slots($id) with an hash mapping slot numbers
58	# to node IDs.
59	#
60	# This function is called when a new Cluster client is initialized
61	# and every time we get a -MOVED redirection error.
62	proc ::valkey_cluster::__method__refresh_nodes_map {id} {
63	    # Contact the first responding startup node.
64	    set idx 0; # Index of the node that will respond.
65	    set errmsg {}
66	    foreach start_node $::valkey_cluster::startup_nodes($id) {
67	        set ip_port [lindex [split $start_node @] 0]
68	        lassign [split $ip_port :] start_host start_port
69	        set tls $::valkey_cluster::tls($id)
70	        if {[catch {
71	            set r {}
72	            set r [valkey $start_host $start_port 0 $tls]
73	            set nodes_descr [$r cluster nodes]
74	            $r close
75	        } e]} {
76	            if {$r ne {}} {catch {$r close}}
77	            incr idx
78	            if {[string length $errmsg] < 200} {
79	                append errmsg " $ip_port: $e"
80	            }
81	            continue ; # Try next.
82	        } else {
83	            break; # Good node found.
84	        }
85	    }
86	
87	    if {$idx == [llength $::valkey_cluster::startup_nodes($id)]} {
88	        error "No good startup node found. $errmsg"
89	    }
90	
91	    # Put the node that responded as first in the list if it is not
92	    # already the first.
93	    if {$idx != 0} {
94	        set l $::valkey_cluster::startup_nodes($id)
95	        set left [lrange $l 0 [expr {$idx-1}]]
96	        set right [lrange $l [expr {$idx+1}] end]
97	        set l [concat [lindex $l $idx] $left $right]
98	        set ::valkey_cluster::startup_nodes($id) $l
99	    }
100	
101	    # Parse CLUSTER NODES output to populate the nodes description.
102	    set nodes {} ; # addr -> node description hash.
103	    foreach line [split $nodes_descr "\n"] {
104	        set line [string trim $line]
105	        if {$line eq {}} continue
106	        set args [split $line " "]
107	        lassign $args nodeid addr flags slaveof pingsent pongrecv configepoch linkstate
108	        set slots [lrange $args 8 end]
109	        set addr [lindex [split $addr @] 0]
110	        if {$addr eq {:0}} {
111	            set addr $start_host:$start_port
112	        }
113	        lassign [split $addr :] host port
114	
115	        # Connect to the node
116	        set link {}
117	        set tls $::valkey_cluster::tls($id)
118	        catch {set link [valkey $host $port 0 $tls]}
119	
120	        # Build this node description as an hash.
121	        set node [dict create \
122	            id $nodeid \

124	            addr $addr \
125	            host $host \
126	            port $port \
127	            flags $flags \
128	            slaveof $slaveof \
129	            slots $slots \
130	            link $link \
131	        ]
132	        dict set nodes $addr $node
133	        lappend ::valkey_cluster::startup_nodes($id) $addr
134	    }

137	    # map as current.

# Tcl cluster client as a wrapper of redis.rb.
# Copyright (c) 2014 Redis Ltd.
# Released under the BSD license like Redis itself
#
# Example usage:
#
# set c [valkey_cluster {127.0.0.1:6379 127.0.0.1:6380}]
# $c set foo
# $c get foo
# $c close

package require Tcl 8.5
package provide valkey_cluster 0.1

namespace eval valkey_cluster {}
set ::valkey_cluster::internal_id 0
set ::valkey_cluster::id 0
array set ::valkey_cluster::startup_nodes {}
array set ::valkey_cluster::nodes {}
array set ::valkey_cluster::slots {}
array set ::valkey_cluster::tls {}

# List of "plain" commands, which are commands where the sole key is always
# the first argument.
set ::valkey_cluster::plain_commands {
    get set setnx setex psetex append strlen exists setbit getbit
    setrange getrange substr incr decr rpush lpush rpushx lpushx
    linsert rpop lpop brpop llen lindex lset lrange ltrim lrem
    sadd srem sismember smismember scard spop srandmember smembers sscan zadd
    zincrby zrem zremrangebyscore zremrangebyrank zremrangebylex zrange
    zrangebyscore zrevrangebyscore zrangebylex zrevrangebylex zcount
    zlexcount zrevrange zcard zscore zmscore zrank zrevrank zscan hset hsetnx
    hget hmset hmget hincrby hincrbyfloat hdel hlen hkeys hvals
    hgetall hexists hscan incrby decrby incrbyfloat getset move
    expire expireat pexpire pexpireat type ttl pttl persist restore
    dump bitcount bitpos pfadd pfcount cluster ssubscribe spublish
    sunsubscribe
}

# Create a cluster client. The nodes are given as a list of host:port. The TLS
# parameter (1 or 0) is optional and defaults to the global $::tls.
proc valkey_cluster {nodes {tls -1}} {
    set id [incr ::valkey_cluster::id]
    set ::valkey_cluster::startup_nodes($id) $nodes
    set ::valkey_cluster::nodes($id) {}
    set ::valkey_cluster::slots($id) {}
    set ::valkey_cluster::tls($id) [expr $tls == -1 ? $::tls : $tls]
    set handle [interp alias {} ::valkey_cluster::instance$id {} ::valkey_cluster::__dispatch__ $id]
    $handle refresh_nodes_map
    return $handle
}

# Totally reset the slots / nodes state for the client, calls
# CLUSTER NODES in the first startup node available, populates the
# list of nodes ::valkey_cluster::nodes($id) with an hash mapping node
# ip:port to a representation of the node (another hash), and finally
# maps ::valkey_cluster::slots($id) with an hash mapping slot numbers
# to node IDs.
#
# This function is called when a new Cluster client is initialized
# and every time we get a -MOVED redirection error.
proc ::valkey_cluster::__method__refresh_nodes_map {id} {
    # Contact the first responding startup node.
    set idx 0; # Index of the node that will respond.
    set errmsg {}
    foreach start_node $::valkey_cluster::startup_nodes($id) {
        set ip_port [lindex [split $start_node @] 0]
        lassign [split $ip_port :] start_host start_port
        set tls $::valkey_cluster::tls($id)
        if {[catch {
            set r {}
            set r [valkey $start_host $start_port 0 $tls]
            set nodes_descr [$r cluster nodes]
            $r close
        } e]} {
            if {$r ne {}} {catch {$r close}}
            incr idx
            if {[string length $errmsg] < 200} {
                append errmsg " $ip_port: $e"
            }
            continue ; # Try next.
        } else {
            break; # Good node found.
        }
    }

    if {$idx == [llength $::valkey_cluster::startup_nodes($id)]} {
        error "No good startup node found. $errmsg"
    }

    # Put the node that responded as first in the list if it is not
    # already the first.
    if {$idx != 0} {
        set l $::valkey_cluster::startup_nodes($id)
        set left [lrange $l 0 [expr {$idx-1}]]
        set right [lrange $l [expr {$idx+1}] end]
        set l [concat [lindex $l $idx] $left $right]
        set ::valkey_cluster::startup_nodes($id) $l
    }

    # Parse CLUSTER NODES output to populate the nodes description.
    set nodes {} ; # addr -> node description hash.
    foreach line [split $nodes_descr "\n"] {
        set line [string trim $line]
        if {$line eq {}} continue
        set args [split $line " "]
        lassign $args nodeid addr flags slaveof pingsent pongrecv configepoch linkstate
        set slots [lrange $args 8 end]
        set addr [lindex [split $addr @] 0]
        if {$addr eq {:0}} {
            set addr $start_host:$start_port
        }
        lassign [split $addr :] host port

        # Connect to the node
        set link {}
        set tls $::valkey_cluster::tls($id)
        catch {set link [valkey $host $port 0 $tls]}

        # Build this node description as an hash.
        set node [dict create \
            id $nodeid \
            internal_id $id \
            addr $addr \
            host $host \
            port $port \
            flags $flags \
            slaveof $slaveof \
            slots $slots \
            link $link \
        ]
        dict set nodes $addr $node
        lappend ::valkey_cluster::startup_nodes($id) $addr
    }

    # Close all the existing links in the old nodes map, and set the new
    # map as current.
    foreach n $::valkey_cluster::nodes($id) {
        catch {
            [dict get $n link] close

content.content: M src/cluster.c

content.text:
Implemented the fix in `src/cluster.c`.

Summary:
- `CONFIG RESETSTAT` already calls `resetClusterStats()`.
- `resetClusterStats()` was only resetting slot statistics via `clusterSlotStatResetAll()`.
- Updated it to also zero:
  - `server.cluster->stats_bus_messages_sent[]`
  - `server.cluster->stats_bus_messages_received[]`

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression test in `tests/unit/cluster/info.tcl` to confirm `CONFIG RESETSTAT` clears cluster bus message totals.
- Built the project with `make -j2`.
- Ran `./runtest --single unit/cluster/info --verbose`.
- The test initially failed before rebuilding/with the bug behavior, then passed after the fix.
- Removed the temporary test with `git restore -- tests/unit/cluster/info.tcl` as requested.

Current working tree:
- Only `src/cluster.c` is modified.

result:
Implemented the fix in `src/cluster.c`.

Summary:
- `CONFIG RESETSTAT` already calls `resetClusterStats()`.
- `resetClusterStats()` was only resetting slot statistics via `clusterSlotStatResetAll()`.
- Updated it to also zero:
  - `server.cluster->stats_bus_messages_sent[]`
  - `server.cluster->stats_bus_messages_received[]`

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression test in `tests/unit/cluster/info.tcl` to confirm `CONFIG RESETSTAT` clears cluster bus message totals.
- Built the project with `make -j2`.
- Ran `./runtest --single unit/cluster/info --verbose`.
- The test initially failed before rebuilding/with the bug behavior, then passed after the fix.
- Removed the temporary test with `git restore -- tests/unit/cluster/info.tcl` as requested.

Current working tree:
- Only `src/cluster.c` is modified.
